title: The 'ABC' Rule: Always place your baby Alone, on their Back, in a Crib or bassinet.
Firm Surfaces Only: Use a firm, flat, non-inclined mattress.
Clear the Crib: No pillows, blankets, toys, or bumpers.
Watch 'Wake Windows': Newborns are usually only awake for 45–60 minutes at a time.
Drowsy but Awake: Put the baby down before they are fully asleep to help them learn to self-soothe.
Blackout Curtains: Use these to help the baby distinguish between day and night.
White Noise: Use a sound machine to mimic the sounds of the womb.
Follow Hunger Cues: Feed when they show signs of hunger (rooting, sucking on hands).
Cluster Feeding: It is normal for newborns to do several short feedings over a few hours.
Growth Spurt Rule (3-6-9): Expect spurts around 3, 6, and 9 weeks, and 3, 6, and 9 months.
Pace the Bottle: Keep the baby upright during bottle-feeding to prevent drinking too fast.
The 'One-Size Up' Trick: If you have frequent leaks, move up a diaper size.
Fluff the Frills: Always pull out the leg ruffles on a diaper to prevent leaks.
Umbilical Cord Care: Fold the front of the diaper down so it doesn't rub the cord stump.
The Onesie Hack: Use 'envelope' shoulders to pull messy onesies down over the legs.
Point Down: For boys, point the penis downward when diapering to prevent leaks.
Skin Peeling: Newborns often shed their outer skin layer in the first week; this is normal.
The 5 S’s: To calm a fussy baby, try Swaddle, Side/Stomach, Shush, Sway, and Suck.
Skin-to-Skin: Holding your baby against your bare chest regulates their heart rate.
Talk and Sing: Narrate your day to help with your baby’s language development.
The 5-8-5 Method: Walk for 5 mins, hold for 8 mins, then lay them down.
Wash Your Hands: Ask everyone to wash their hands before touching the baby.
Support the Neck: Always cradle the head and neck when carrying your baby.
Check the Water: Set your water heater to 120°F (48°C) to prevent bath burns.
Fever Protocol: Use a rectal thermometer for the most accurate reading in infants.
The 5-5-5 Rule: 5 days in bed, 5 days around the bed, and 5 days around the house.
Sleep When the Baby Sleeps: Prioritize rest over non-essential chores.
Accept Help: If someone offers to bring a meal or do laundry, say yes.
Reset the Bag: Refill your diaper bag as soon as you get home so it’s always ready.
